annotations.label: Metric space

anchor.snippet: a metric space is a set where a notion of distance

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `class MetricSpace` capturing a set with a distance function satisfying the metric axioms.

annotations.label: Formal definition of metric space

anchor.snippet: Formally, a metric space is an ordered pair

annotations.note: Mathlib's `MetricSpace` class encodes the same ordered-pair (M, d) data together with the three metric axioms.

annotations.label: Non-negativity of a metric

anchor.snippet: By taking the third property

annotations.note: `dist_nonneg : 0 ≤ dist x y` is proved at line 251 of `Mathlib/Topology/MetricSpace/Pseudo/Defs.lean`.

annotations.label: Sequence

anchor.snippet: A sequence is an ordered list

annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes sequences both as functions `ℕ → α` (used throughout analysis) and as `Stream'.Seq` for possibly-finite ordered lists.

annotations.label: Convergence of a sequence

anchor.snippet: One of the most important properties of a sequence is convergence

annotations.note: Sequence convergence is expressed via `Filter.Tendsto u Filter.atTop (𝓝 x)` using Mathlib's filter-based limits.

annotations.label: Continuous function at a point

anchor.snippet: is continuous at a point

annotations.note: `ContinuousAt f x` is defined in Mathlib as `Filter.Tendsto f (𝓝 x) (𝓝 (f x))`.

annotations.label: Real analysis

anchor.snippet: Real analysis (traditionally, the "theory of functions of a real variable")

annotations.note: Real analysis is a subject-area label, not a single formal definition; Mathlib develops it in `Mathlib/Analysis/` but does not formalize the meta-notion itself.

annotations.label: Complex analysis

anchor.snippet: Complex analysis (traditionally known as the "theory of functions of a complex variable")

annotations.note: Complex analysis is a discipline name rather than a formal statement; while Mathlib has extensive complex-analysis content, the term itself is not formalized.

annotations.label: Functional analysis

anchor.snippet: Functional analysis is a branch of mathematical analysis

annotations.note: Functional analysis is a subject-area label; Mathlib formalizes its objects (e.g., `NormedSpace`) but not the discipline itself.

annotations.label: Free (noncommutative) analysis

anchor.snippet: Free or noncommutative analysis is a sub-branch of functional analysis

annotations.note: No formalization of free/noncommutative analysis as a topic was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Harmonic analysis

anchor.snippet: Harmonic analysis is a branch of mathematical analysis concerned with the representation of functions and signals

annotations.note: Harmonic analysis is a discipline label, not a formal definition; Mathlib has Fourier-analysis content but does not formalize the umbrella term.

annotations.label: Differential equation

anchor.snippet: A differential equation is a mathematical equation for an unknown function

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Mathlib/Analysis/ODE/` formalizes ODE solutions (e.g., Picard–Lindelöf, Gronwall), but there is no general `DifferentialEquation` definition.

annotations.label: Measure on a set

anchor.snippet: A measure on a set is a systematic way to assign a number

annotations.note: `MeasureTheory.Measure` is Mathlib's formalization of a measure on a measurable space.

annotations.label: Technical definition of a measure

anchor.snippet: Technically, a measure is a function that assigns a non-negative real number

annotations.note: Mathlib's `MeasureTheory.Measure` encodes the countably-additive non-negative set function with the standard axioms.

annotations.label: Lebesgue measure of an interval

anchor.snippet: For instance, the Lebesgue measure of the interval

annotations.note: `Real.volume_Ioo`/`volume_Icc` give the Lebesgue measure of an interval as `ENNReal.ofReal (b - a)`.

annotations.label: Numerical analysis

anchor.snippet: Numerical analysis is the study of algorithms that use numerical approximation

annotations.note: Numerical analysis is a subject-area description, not a formal mathematical definition, and is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Vector analysis

anchor.snippet: Vector analysis , also called vector calculus , is a branch of mathematical analysis

annotations.note: Vector analysis is a discipline name; Mathlib has the relevant objects (grad, div, curl-type operators) but not the term itself.

annotations.label: Scalar analysis

anchor.snippet: Scalar analysis is a branch of mathematical analysis dealing with values related to scale

annotations.note: Not a formal mathematical definition; no Mathlib formalization found.

annotations.label: Calculus of variations

anchor.snippet: Calculus of variations deals with extremizing functionals

annotations.note: Calculus of variations is a subject-area label and is not formalized as a definition in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Harmonic analysis (other topics)

anchor.snippet: Harmonic analysis deals with the representation of functions or signals

annotations.note: A duplicate subject-area description; not formalized as a definition in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Geometric analysis

anchor.snippet: Geometric analysis involves the use of geometrical methods

annotations.note: Geometric analysis is a discipline name and is not formalized as such.

annotations.label: Clifford analysis

anchor.snippet: Clifford analysis , the study of Clifford valued functions

annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes `CliffordAlgebra` but not Clifford analysis (analytic study of Clifford-valued functions) as a discipline.

annotations.label: p-adic analysis

anchor.snippet: -adic analysis , the study of analysis within the context of

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `Padic` (ℚ_p) and `PadicInt`, providing the setting for p-adic analysis, but p-adic analysis itself is not a formal definition.

annotations.label: Non-standard analysis

anchor.snippet: Non-standard analysis , which investigates the hyperreal numbers

annotations.note: Mathlib has some hyperreals/ultrafilters infrastructure but does not formalize non-standard analysis as a subject.

annotations.label: Computable analysis

anchor.snippet: Computable analysis , the study of which parts of analysis can be carried out

annotations.note: Computable analysis is a subject-area and is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Stochastic calculus

anchor.snippet: Stochastic calculus – analytical notions developed for stochastic processes

annotations.note: Mathlib has probability-theory content but not stochastic calculus as a formalized topic.

annotations.label: Set-valued analysis

anchor.snippet: Set-valued analysis – applies ideas from analysis and topology

annotations.note: Set-valued analysis is a discipline label and is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Convex analysis

anchor.snippet: Convex analysis , the study of convex sets and functions

annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes `Convex` sets and `ConvexOn` functions in `Mathlib/Analysis/Convex/`, but not 'convex analysis' as a discipline.

annotations.label: Idempotent analysis

anchor.snippet: Idempotent analysis – analysis in the context of an idempotent semiring

annotations.note: Idempotent analysis is not formalized as a topic in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Constructive analysis

anchor.snippet: Constructive analysis , which is built upon a foundation of constructive

annotations.note: Constructive analysis as a discipline is not formalized in Mathlib (Mathlib uses classical logic).

annotations.label: Intuitionistic analysis

anchor.snippet: Intuitionistic analysis , which is developed from constructive logic

annotations.note: Intuitionistic analysis is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Paraconsistent analysis

anchor.snippet: Paraconsistent analysis , which is built upon a foundation of paraconsistent

annotations.note: Paraconsistent analysis is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Smooth infinitesimal analysis

anchor.snippet: Smooth infinitesimal analysis , which is developed in a smooth topos

annotations.note: Smooth infinitesimal analysis is not formalized in Mathlib.
